The Problem

The Question No HR System
Has Ever Answered

<25%

Global Employee Engagement Rate

Unchanged for two decades — despite hundreds of billions invested in HR technology. The problem is not lack of data. It is asking the wrong question. Every HR tool measures what people do. None measure why they do it.

HRIS Systems

Record what happened. Administrative ledgers — not intelligence. No signal on purpose, meaning, or motivation whatsoever.

Engagement Surveys

Measure satisfaction 1–4× per year. Scores, not understanding. Too infrequent, too aggregated, no action path for leadership.

People Analytics

Retrospective workforce data — rearview mirrors. Cannot model purpose alignment because purpose is not a variable in their schemas.

The Solution

Kaika AI — The Ikigai Intelligence Platform

By translating Japan's Ikigai philosophy into continuous AI-driven organizational intelligence, Kaika AI creates an entirely new category: Purpose-Driven Workforce Intelligence.

What You Love

Passion & Vocation — what brings intrinsic motivation to daily work

IKIGAI
生き甲斐

What You're Good At

Strengths & Mission — where capability meets organizational need

What the World Needs

Purpose & Mission — the connection between role and broader meaning

What You're Rewarded For

Vocation & Profession — sustainable alignment between contribution and compensation

Purpose Alignment Score

Individual & team-level Ikigai alignment measurement. Continuous, not annual.

Workforce Intelligence Brief

Weekly AI synthesis of organizational purpose health delivered to leadership.

Purpose Drift Detection

Early warning signals for disengagement — 30–90 days before resignation.

Mission Fit Index

Role-level analysis of alignment between individual purpose and organizational mission.

Recommended Action Layer

Specific, actionable interventions for managers and HR leaders — not just scores.

Organizational Intelligence

Cross-team synthesis. Enterprise-wide view of purpose coherence and cultural alignment.

Japan is the perfect launch market: Ikigai originates here, enterprise HR innovation demand is high, and Japanese corporate culture has deep philosophical alignment with purpose-driven work. A Japan success story becomes the global proof of concept.

Strategic Moat

Why Kaika AI Cannot
Be Easily Replicated

Five self-reinforcing advantages that compound over time — making Kaika AI progressively harder to displace as adoption grows.

Cultural Moat

Ikigai is Japan's philosophical heritage. Authentic cultural foundation that global SaaS competitors cannot simply adopt.

Data Moat

Each organization using Kaika AI contributes to a proprietary workforce purpose dataset — the largest of its kind.

AI Learning Loop

More data → better models → more precise predictions → greater value → more adoption. Compounds with scale.

Network Effects

Industry benchmarking data makes the platform more valuable as corporate adoption increases.

Enterprise Lock-In

Once integrated with HR workflows and data systems, switching costs are prohibitive for enterprise clients.

Category Creation

Category creators capture 70%+ of economic value. Kaika AI defines Purpose-Driven Workforce Intelligence.

The Founder

Nfuankeku Tabia Rene

Founder & CEO, Kaika AI Inc. — Tokyo, Japan

Born in Cameroon, built his professional life in Japan. The combination of technology expertise and deep cultural immersion in the country that gave the world Ikigai positions Tabia uniquely to build this company. His cross-cultural journey revealed the gap that Kaika AI was founded to close: organizations have more data about their people than ever — and understand them less than ever.

Kaika AI is not a market opportunity he identified. It is the answer to a question he has carried for years: why does the most important variable in organizational performance — human purpose — have no intelligence system dedicated to measuring and acting on it?

IT Engineer Entrepreneur Japan Immersed Ikigai Practitioner Category Creator Cameroon → Tokyo
